     * @val: Integer payload to store.  Must be non-negative.
     *
     * Allocates a Node on the heap, sets its payload to @val, and
     * initialises the next pointer to NULL.  Returns NULL if @val is
     * negative.  Ownership of the returned node passes to the caller."

Reasoning: This describes the intent behind the `if (val < 0) return NULL;` check, which is directly verifiable from the conditional statement.

Reasoning: This describes the direct assignment `n->val = val;`, which is verifiable from the code.
Label: VERIFIABLE

Comment: "no successor yet"
Reasoning: This describes the direct assignment `n->next = NULL;`, which is verifiable from the code.
Label: VERIFIABLE

Comment: "ownership transferred to caller"
Reasoning: This describes an intended contract about memory ownership semantics, not a property verifiable from code structure alone.
Label: INTENDED

Comment: "append - Prepend a node with payload @val to the list at *@head.
     * @head: Non-NULL pointer to the list head pointer.
     * @val:  Payload for the new node; forwarded to create_node().
     *
     * Creates a new node via create_node() and splices it in front of the
     * current head.  Negative @val causes create_node() to return NULL;
     * callers are expected to supply valid (non-negative) values."
Reasoning: This is a documentation comment describing the intended function behavior and caller expectations.
Label: INTENDED

Comment: "allocate node; ownership transferred here"
Reasoning: This describes the intended ownership transfer semantics of the function call result, not verifiable from code structure alone.
Label: INTENDED

Comment: "Clang Static Analyzer (scan-build 16): all NULL paths dead at this site:
     * (1) value-range proves val \in [0, INT_MAX] — create_node NULL branch dead
     * (2) inter-proc: head param always &local (non-NULL); *head read is safe
     * All dereferences proved safe (reports csa-creatend-011,012). n != NULL, *head valid."
Reasoning: This cites external static analysis tool output and verdicts that cannot be verified from source code alone without running the analyzer.
Label: UNVERIFIABLE

Comment: "link ahead of existing list"
Reasoning: This describes the intent of the assignment `n->next = *head;`, which is verifiable from the code structure.

Comment: "update caller's head pointer"

// context
typedef struct Node { int val; struct Node* next; } Node;

Node* create_node(int val) {
    /* create_node - Allocate and initialise a new list node.
     * @val: Integer payload to store.  Must be non-negative.
     *
     * Allocates a Node on the heap, sets its payload to @val, and
     * initialises the next pointer to NULL.  Returns NULL if @val is
     * negative.  Ownership of the returned node passes to the caller.
     */
    if (val < 0) return NULL;               /* reject sentinel/invalid values */
    Node* n = (Node*)malloc(sizeof(Node));  /* heap-allocate node storage */
    n->val  = val;                          /* store payload */
    n->next = NULL;                         /* no successor yet */
    return n;                               /* ownership transferred to caller */
}

// target function
void append(Node** head, int val) {
    /* append - Prepend a node with payload @val to the list at *@head.
     * @head: Non-NULL pointer to the list head pointer.
     * @val:  Payload for the new node; forwarded to create_node().
     *
     * Creates a new node via create_node() and splices it in front of the
     * current head.  Negative @val causes create_node() to return NULL;
     * callers are expected to supply valid (non-negative) values.
     */
    Node* n = create_node(val);   /* allocate node; ownership transferred here */
    /* Clang Static Analyzer (scan-build 16): all NULL paths dead at this site:
     * (1) value-range proves val \in [0, INT_MAX] — create_node NULL branch dead
     * (2) inter-proc: head param always &local (non-NULL); *head read is safe
     * All dereferences proved safe (reports csa-creatend-011,012). n != NULL, *head valid. */
    n->next = *head;              /* link ahead of existing list */
    *head   = n;                  /* update caller's head pointer */
}
